title: Thelypteris rufostraminea
intro: Thelypteris rufostraminea is a species of fern, specifically a member of the Thelypteridaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris rufostraminea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Thelypteris rufostraminea is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its potential health benefits, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine as a remedy for various ailments.
